title: phlegmariurus cuernavacensis
intro: Phlegmariurus cuernavacensis, a type of epiphytic or terrestrial plant native to Mexico, is a species of the Phlegmariurus genus, classified as a type of club moss or lycopod. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Phlegmariurus cuernavacensis include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The herbal preparations typically involve the plant's leaves and stems, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
